column3: A human-made work of art created using paint on a prepared support; the central entity in this model.
Document Note: Heritage Object Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Heritage Object Model Overview
column3: This model defines the Heritage Object (E22) as the core object from which samples, analytical data, and descriptive documentation are derived. It records ownership, collection context, dimensions, support, and medium, linking the artwork to its production event, associated documentation, and any related scientific or curatorial information. Together, these relationships provide a foundation for integrating technical study, provenance, and contextual research across connected datasets.
The legal owner or holding institution of the painting.

column1: //links E39: Institution or Person --> person, institution

column3: The institution or person currently responsible for the painting’s care or location.
The physical location or site where the painting is housed or displayed.
The measured height, width, and optionally depth of the painting, recorded as dimensional attributes.
The collection or sub-collection to which the painting formally belongs.
Any document, catalogue entry, publication, or report relating to the painting’s description, study, or conservation.
The material or binding medium used to form the paint layers of the artwork.
Any material forming part of the original composition or manufacture of a heritage object or sample, beyond media or supports.
The physical base material or structure on which the paint was applied (e.g., canvas, panel, metal).
The creative or manufacturing event during which the painting was produced or completed.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
Can include a whole range of descriptions, reports, notes, publications etc.
A wide range of different additional identifiers can be associated to painting from historic codes to other globally resolvable PIDs.
Multiple values selected from a defined controlled list.
The current condition, treatment state, or lifecycle position of a heritage object, conservation history, handling, availability, or degree of alteration over time.
